solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int numMusicPlaylists(int n, int goal, int k) {
        const int MOD = 1e9 + 7;
        vector<vector<int>> dp(goal + 1, vector<int>(n + 1, 0));
        dp[0][0] = 1;
        
        for (int i = 1; i <= goal; ++i) {
            for (int j = 1; j <= n; ++j) {
                dp[i][j] = (dp[i][j - 1] * (n - (j - 1))) % MOD;
                if (j > k) {
                    dp[i][j] = (dp[i][j] + dp[i - 1][j - 1]) % MOD;
                } else if (j > 1) {
                    dp[i][j] = (dp[i][j] + dp[i - 1][j - 1]) % MOD;
                }
            }
        }
        
        return dp[goal][n];
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(goal * n)
space complexity: O(goal * n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution uses dynamic programming to count the number of valid playlists. The `dp[i][j]` represents the number of playlists of length `i` that contain exactly `j` unique songs. The recurrence considers adding a new song or reusing an old one while respecting the `k` constraint. The key strength is the efficient use of DP to avoid redundant computation, while the main weakness is the O(goal * n) space requirement.
